title: METR releases its first Frontier Risk Report, a pilot assessment of rogue deployment risks at leading AI labs including Anthropic, Google DeepMind, Meta, and OpenAI covering February to March 2026 · Digg
summary: METR releases its first Frontier Risk Report, a pilot assessment of rogue deployment risks at leading AI labs including Anthropic, Google DeepMind, Meta, and OpenAI covering February to March 2026 · Digg Published: 2026-05-19T18:11:53+00:00 Summary METR has released its first Frontier Risk R
content: METR releases its first Frontier Risk Report, a pilot assessment of rogue deployment risks at leading AI labs including Anthropic, Google DeepMind, Meta, and OpenAI [...] March 20 [...] 6 · Digg [...] METR has released its first Frontier Risk Report, a pilot assessment of rogue deployment risks at leading AI labs including Anthropic, Google DeepMind, Meta, and OpenAI, covering February to March 2026. The report is led by METR's lead writer, Rob Wood, who led the writing for the report. The work is the culmination of years of effort on AI evaluation science and third-party risk assessment and disclosure mechanism design. The study involved testing of Anthropic's best internal models with CoT access and reviewing non-public info about capabilities, alignment, and control. It also involved significantly deeper access than previously had, including raw chains-of-thought from developers' best models and information about private model training & control protocols. However, the report lacks quantitative risk estimates, which could lead to incorrect impressions of the report's implications.

title: WaveSpeed Expands Unified LLM API With Access to GPT, Claude, Gemini and 260+ Models
summary: WaveSpeed Expands Unified LLM API With Access to GPT, Claude, Gemini and 260+ Models Published: 2026-05-17T10:01:00-04:00 Type: Press Release Summary WaveSpeed has expanded its unified LLM API to allow developers to access more than 260 language models, including GPT, Claude, Gemini, Grok, D
content: WaveSpeed has expanded its unified LLM API to allow developers to access more than 260 language models, including GPT, Claude, Gemini, Grok, DeepSeek, Llama, Qwen and Mistral. The new API allows developers to compare, switch and deploy leading LLMs while connecting language workflows with image, video, audio and 3D generation. Unlike LLM-only gateways, WaveSpeed connects language models with a broader catalog of over 1,000 total AI models. The API uses a standard chat-completions interface and includes support for chat completions, streaming, JSON mode, tool use and vision. Pricing is transparent and per-token, with separate input and output rates listed for each model.

title: Karpathy, Who Called Today's AI Agents 'Slop,' Joins Anthropic to Use Claude to Build the Next Claude
summary: Karpathy, Who Called Today's AI Agents 'Slop,' Joins Anthropic to Use Claude to Build the Next Claude Published: 2026-05-19T14:56:50-04:00 Author: Tom K. Published: May 19 2026, 14:56 PM EDT Summary Andrej Karpathy, an OpenAI founding member and researcher, has joined Anthropic from OpenAI t
content: ,' Joins Anthropic to Use [...] Andrej Karpathy, an OpenAI founding member and researcher, has joined Anthropic from OpenAI to help develop the next version of Claude. He will be on the company's pre-training team under team lead Nick Joseph and will immediately build a new group with a mandate to use Claude to speed up research. Pre-training, the first and largest phase of building a large language model, is responsible for the massive training runs that give Claude its core knowledge and capabilities. This phase, which traditionally requires significant human research time, will replace this with autonomous research loops running overnight, proposing and evaluating changes to training code without intervention. Karpath's own tool, autoresearch, demonstrated that autoreSearch could improve training at small scale. title: Google unveils AI model Gemini 3.5 and AI agent Gemini Spark
summary: Google unveils AI model Gemini 3.5 and AI agent Gemini Spark Key Points - Google is showcasing Gemini 3.5 Flash, a lighter-weight addition to its suite that offers cutting-edge capabilities. - The company announced a new world model called Omni.
content: Google unveils AI model Gemini 3.5 and AI agent Gemini Spark [...] - Google is showcasing Gemini 3.5 Flash, a lighter-weight addition to its suite that offers cutting-edge capabilities. - The company announced a new world model called Omni. - Google is racing to keep up with OpenAI and Anthropic while also rolling out more services to its expansive user base. [...] Google is rolling out its latest version of Gemini and a new artificial intelligence model designed to simulate the physical world, as the search giant races to keep pace in model development while also providing more agentic services to its massive user base. [...] The company made the announcements at its annual Google I/O developer conference on Tuesday, gaining an audience for new product debuts at a time when the market has been focused on the soaring valuations of OpenAI and Anthropic, which are both gearing up for IPOs as soon as this year. [...] The centerpiece of Google’s AI strategy is Gemini, its family of models and tools. The company is showcasing Gemini 3.5 Flash, a lighter-weight addition to its suite that offers cutting-edge capabilities at half, or in some cases close to one-third, the price of comparable frontier models, according to CEO Sundar Pichai. [...] In a news briefing with reporters ahead of Tuesday’s event, Pichai said Gemini 3.5 Flash is “remarkably fast.” The company said 3.5 Flash will now be the default model for the Gemini app and AI mode in search globally. [...] On the agentic AI front, Google announced Gemini Spark, a new general-purpose AI agent in the Gemini app that can reason across information in connected apps. Google said it wants to help users navigate their digital lives by taking “action on your behalf while under your direction.” Gemini Spark is in beta and will be available first to trusted testers and Google AI Ultra subscribers, starting next week. [...] Google’s AI portfolio now includes Omni, a world model designed to simulate physical environments, predicting what happens next based on a user’s actions. World models are often used in robotics and gaming and have been heavily researched by DeepMind through the years. [...] Omni will work in Flash, the Gemini App, Google Flow and YouTube shorts, supporting image and audio, the company said, adding in a separate blog post that users can have Omni edit videos and create more realistic imagery. [...] “Take a video you shot and just ask Omni to change what’s happening,” the post says. The AI can “edit the action, add in new characters or objects.”

title: Google Unveils Gemini Omni—A Next-Gen AI Video Builder That Can 'Simulate the World'
summary: Google Unveils Gemini Omni—A Next-Gen AI Video Builder That Can 'Simulate the World' - Decrypt Published: 2026-05-19T19:26:50 Author: Decrypt / Jason Nelson Summary Google has introduced Gemini Omni, a new multimodal AI model that combines the company's Gemini AI models with its media-genera
content: Google has introduced Gemini Omni, a new multimodal AI model that combines the company's Gemini AI models with its media-generation tools, including Veo, Nano Banana, and Genie. The announcement came during Google I/O 2026, where DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis described Gemini Omni as a new model that can create anything from any input. The first release, Gemini Omni Flash, will launch through Flow, the company’s AI filmmaking platform, and Flow Music, which focuses on AI-assisted music creation. title: Announcing Claude Managed Agents on Cloudflare
summary: Announcing Claude Managed Agents on Cloudflare Published: 2026-05-19T14:00:00+01:00 Summary Cloudflare and Anthropic have collaborated to integrate Claude Managed Agents with Cloudflare Sandboxes. This gives developers more control over agent sandboxes, secure connections to private services
content: Cloudflare and Anthropic have collaborated to integrate Claude Managed Agents with Cloudflare Sandboxes. This gives developers more control over agent sandboxes, secure connections to private services, and improves observability. The integration also allows users to run their agent loop on the Claude Platform while using CloudFlare to execute code, secure connects, and run custom tool calls. The integrated integration includes enhanced security, private service connectivity, sandbox control and observability, and custom tools. This integration allows developers to easily define and run agents on the Anthropic platform. The core agent loop runs in Anthropic, but the infrastructure for running and executing code can be run anywhere. The new integration gives your agents a Cloud-based environment within minutes.
